confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_32c66b5f-c096-4b19-a671-a1129ababdb7 read back as received

evidence: Send-OperatorSurfaceIntent nudge narada-cpy.builder failed with unknown_operator_surface_target.
Direct Send-OperatorSurfaceInput failed with submit_strategy_unavailable: no enabled known_surface_submit strategy for narada-cpy.builder.
narada task peek-next --agent narada-cpy.builder failed with agent_not_in_roster and repair_command narada task roster add <agent-id> --role builder.
After adding aliases, submit strategy, and task roster entry, the nudge submitted successfully to HWND 3346276 and restored previous focus.
proposal: Agent/operator-surface admission should either populate all required projections for the requested capabilities, or emit a bounded readiness checklist with exact commands for alias, submit-strategy, runtime binding, and task-roster completion.

Role-shaped identities admitted for a Site builder should be registered consistently across operator-surface identity, message aliases, submit strategy, and task roster when those capabilities are requested.
payload.recommendation: Create a Narada proper task to make operator-surface agent instantiate/admit produce a complete ready-to-nudge builder surface for client Sites.
payload.summary: After admitting narada-cpy.builder, the identity existed in the User Site registry and could be labeled, but architect could not nudge it through the normal operator-surface intent path. The alias layer did not resolve narada-cpy.builder, the submit-strategy registry had no entry for it, and CPY task peek-next refused the same identity because it was not in the task roster. The architect manually added User Site input aliases, a submit strategy, and a CPY task roster entry before nudge worked.
payload.title: Operator-surface identity admission should complete messaging and task-roster readiness
